baseline_reviewed: RC5.2 current checkout; plan rev-6 (2595 lines)
prior_verdicts_in_chain: .review.json — NO_GO_REV_3 four-axis review; systems contributed SYS-1 (handlers), SYS-2 (StaleComposeStateError contract), SYS-3 (literal call-site enumeration), plus WARN-SYS-3 (audit-access-log reinforcing loop) and WARN-SYS-4 (CL-PP-10c property-only)
Reality cleared at rev-3 and rev-4 changes don't materially touch reality-axis surfaces
summary: Five of seven prior systems concerns are closed in rev-6. WARN-SYS-3 is partially addressed (counter + runbook deliverable; feedback loop made observable but not structurally broken; Phase 4 owns the frontend retry contract). One new high-severity defect surfaced: the AuditIntegrityError.failed_turn invariant is stated as unconditionally required and the FastAPI handler accesses exc.failed_turn directly, but ~20 existing AuditIntegrityError raise sites in Phase 1 code do not populate the field. If any of those raise sites is reachable at the compose endpoint handler, the handler crashes with AttributeError and FastAPI returns a generic 500 body — exactly the SYS-1/Q3-2 defect class recurring on a third adjacent surface. The plan also forbids the getattr fallback pattern that would make the invariant survivable (rev-5 B-QA-3 fix). This is the same recurrence pattern that produced line-102 (architecture) and line-105 (quality) ground rules, now manifesting on the systems axis.

SYS-1_FastAPI_handler_registration.notes: Closed by rev-4; intact through rev-5 and rev-6. Task 7b registers three handlers (AuditIntegrityError, StaleComposeStateError, AuditAccessLogWriteError). Convergent with rev-3 quality Q3-2.

SYS-2_StaleComposeStateError_route_contr.notes: Closed by rev-4; intact through rev-6. Handler at plan line 1349 returns 409 with error_type='stale_compose_state'. Uses exc.session_id directly, no getattr.

SYS-3_literal_call_site_enumeration.notes: Closed by rev-4; intact through rev-6. Task 6 table at plan lines 1027-1039 enumerates all 9 routes.py line numbers (1364, 1514, 1737, 3444, 3986, 4944, 4964, 5533, 5553) with per-row disposition.

WARN_SYS_3_audit_access_log_reinforcing_.notes: Rev-6 delivers audit_access_log_write_failed_total counter and an in-PR runbook at docs/runbooks/audit-tier1-violation.md. The reinforcing feedback loop itself is documented as a triage concern but not structurally broken (no frontend backoff specified, no circuit-breaker on record_audit_grade_view_async path). Phase 4 owns the frontend retry contract. Residual warning, not blocker — the plan does not claim to prevent the loop, only to make it observable.

WARN_SYS_4_CL_PP_10c_integration_pin.notes: Rev-6 promotes CL-PP-10c and CL-PP-10d from property-test-only to named integration tests at plan lines 2065 and 2067 with concrete injection mechanisms (after persist_compose_turn_async enters shielded worker; post-COMMIT cancellation window).

blocking_issues.title: AuditIntegrityError.failed_turn invariant is structurally unsatisfiable as specified
blocking_issues.issue: The plan asserts at line 1371 that 'every raise path that can reach FastAPI must populate failed_turn before raising,' and the handler at line 1333 accesses failed_turn = exc.failed_turn directly without fallback. The Phase 1 codebase contains ~20 existing raise AuditIntegrityError(...) sites across src/elspeth/contracts/errors.py, src/elspeth/web/sessions/protocol.py, src/elspeth/web/sessions/service.py, and src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py. None of these populate failed_turn. The rev-6 catch-and-annotate pattern in _compose_loop (plan lines 996-1018) only covers AuditIntegrityError raised by persist_compose_turn_async — the sync-worker path. Other raise sites that reach the compose endpoint's FastAPI handler will cause exc.failed_turn to raise AttributeError, FastAPI falls back to the generic 500 body, and the SYS-1/Q3-2 defect class recurs.
evidence_raise_sites: src/elspeth/contracts/errors.py — AuditIntegrityError class definition has no failed_turn field or constructor parameter (plain Exception subclass)
src/elspeth/web/sessions/protocol.py — multiple raise sites in __post_init__ validation paths (e.g., ChatMessage, SessionRun row validators) fire on session reads
src/elspeth/web/sessions/service.py — raises in SessionServiceImpl methods other than persist_compose_turn_async
src/elspeth/web/sessions/routes.py — raises inside route handlers that propagate directly to FastAPI without passing through _compose_loop's annotation block
blocking_issues.failure_mode: Any AuditIntegrityError originating outside _compose_loop's catch-and-annotate block reaches the FastAPI handler without failed_turn populated → handler crashes at exc.failed_turn → FastAPI generic 500 body → operator sees no diagnostic body → SYS-1 defect class recurs (handler exists but is unusable).
blocking_issues.recurrence_pattern_note: This is the third manifestation of the same defect class: (1) rev-3 SYS-1/Q3-2: handler missing entirely; (2) rev-5 B-QA-3: handler exists but uses defensive getattr to paper over the gap (forbidden by CLAUDE.md); (3) rev-6 NEW-SYS-1: handler uses direct access but the invariant required for direct access is not guaranteed by code structure. The 'FastAPI handler for AuditIntegrityError returns useful diagnostic body' surface has produced one finding per revision for three revisions straight.
blocking_issues.remediation_option_a: Reachability proof. Enumerate the call graph from each non-_compose_loop AuditIntegrityError raise site to the compose endpoint's FastAPI handler. For each, prove either (i) the raise site is not reachable in compose endpoint context, or (ii) earlier route-level handling catches and translates before propagation. If proof completes, current plan is correct. If proof fails, raise sites must be fixed to populate failed_turn at the raise site (not at a catch site). Rigorous but high-effort.
blocking_issues.remediation_option_b: Typed-nullable invariant. Change AuditIntegrityError.failed_turn declaration to failed_turn: FailedTurnMetadata | None = None. The handler accesses exc.failed_turn directly (still no getattr) but branches: if exc.failed_turn is not None, emit the full diagnostic body; if None, emit a typed degraded body (e.g., {'error_type': 'audit_integrity_error', 'diagnostic': 'no_failed_turn_metadata', 'reason': 'AuditIntegrityError originated outside compose-loop annotation scope'}). This is CLAUDE.md-compliant: it's a typed-field direct access with a typed branch, not a defensive getattr. Document the None branch as a known-state assertion at the route boundary — the route layer is asserting 'no failed_turn means non-compose-loop origin', which is offensive programming, not defensive. Small change, structurally robust.
blocking_issues.recommendation: Option (b) — typed-nullable invariant. Reason: option (a) requires a reachability proof the planner cannot easily produce in this iteration, and even if completed it produces a fragile invariant (any future raise site addition breaks the proof). Option (b) makes the invariant survivable by construction. The typed-None branch is distinguishable from rev-5 B-QA-3's forbidden getattr because (i) it accesses the typed field directly via attribute access, (ii) the None state is a documented known state, not an unknown absent state, and (iii) it produces a typed degraded response rather than papering over the gap.

warnings.title: Cancellation arrival-point procedural rule absent
warnings.issue: Rev-6 enumerates nine cancellation arrival points in st_cancellation_arrival_time (plan lines 2173-2181) and maps each to an injection mechanism (lines 2085-2095). The current surface is exhaustively enumerated. However, there is no ground-rule equivalent to line 102 (architecture sweep) or line 105 (quality fixture inventory) that would require a future implementer adding a new await point in _compose_loop to update the cancellation enum and the injection mechanism table. Architecture has line 104; quality has line 105; systems has no procedural rule binding cancellation enumeration to new await points.
warnings.severity_rationale: Low risk for this PR — current surface is complete. Higher risk in Phase 4+ when _compose_loop may acquire new await points (recovery panel state-save, future streaming paths). Advisory only — the operator may add this rule pre-emptively or wait for the recurrence to manifest.
warnings.remediation: Add a one-sentence ground rule alongside line 102 and line 105: 'Every new await point in _compose_loop or in any sync-worker dispatch path requires updating the st_cancellation_arrival_time enum and the injection mechanism table before commit; if the new await point cannot be assigned to one of the existing enum values, add a new value and a concrete injection mechanism, or document why cancellation cannot arrive at that point.'

coherent_surfaces: SYS-1, SYS-2, SYS-3 handler/contract/enumeration surfaces — all closed by rev-4 and intact through rev-6.
WARN-SYS-4 CL-PP-10c integration pin — closed by rev-6 (lines 2065, 2067).
FailedTurnMetadata frozen dataclass — scalar-only fields, no freeze_fields needed; CLAUDE.md compliance correct.
Advisory lock failure mode — enumerated in property test failure-injection arms; injection mechanism present.

Counter wiring with runbook — audit_access_log_write_failed_total + tool_row_tier1_violation_total counters delivered with in-PR runbook; alert routes and dashboard scoped to Filigree follow-up which is the correct disposition for a project without deployed monitoring infra.

cross_axis_status.architecture: GO (cleared at rev-5; line-102 structural ground rule)
cross_axis_status.quality: GO (cleared at rev-6; line-105 structural ground rule)
cross_axis_status.reality: GO (cleared at rev-3; rev-4 through rev-6 changes do not touch reality-axis surfaces)

verdict_next_step: Fix NEW-SYS-1 (option b recommended: typed-nullable failed_turn with typed degraded handler branch). Optionally add ADV-SYS-1 ground rule for cancellation-arrival-point enumeration to close the systems-axis recurrence cycle the same way line 102 closed architecture and line 105 closed quality. Without ADV-SYS-1, rev-8 or later may surface a tenth cancellation arrival point that wasn't enumerated when a future await was added.
recurrence_pattern_summary: Three of four axes now have structural ground rules. Architecture: line 102 signature-change sweep. Quality: line 105 red-test fixture inventory. Systems: none. The three-revision history of the 'FastAPI handler for AuditIntegrityError returns useful diagnostic body' surface (missing handler → defensive getattr → unsatisfied invariant) is exactly the recurrence pattern a systems-axis ground rule would prevent. Reality has no equivalent because reality issues are static — they don't recur on adjacent surfaces the way structural defects do.
